evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: you build transformation models, write dbt tests, and design star and snowflake schemas from scratch
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (dbt tests, reliability, documented requirements) balance against a performance-driven, fast culture framing, giving parity.
evidence.domain_risk: Experience in a B2B SaaS environment with GTM, revenue, or customer journey data
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: B2B SaaS GTM/revenue analytics affects business decisions but no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language is present.
evidence.collaboration_width: analysts; GTM managers; GTM Strategy & Insights managers; engineers
evidence.data_team_maturity: extending our existing dimensional models before progressively taking ownership of new datamart development as our data foundation grows
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The data foundation is actively growing with a lead Data Engineer and named GTM Analytics & Insights team, indicating a mid-stage, scaling function.
evidence.jd_authorship: you build high-performance dashboards and understand data source optimization, calculated fields, and LOD expressions
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Highly precise technical terms (LOD expressions, window functions/CTEs, star/snowflake schema, dbt tests) give a very concrete picture of daily work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: give GTM teams actionable visibility into revenue KPIs and customer journey metrics
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Revenue KPIs, GTM Ops, and customer journey metrics for sales/marketing teams dominate the framing, a clear commercial orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own and extend our dimensional modeling layer — learn our existing dbt models and datamart conventions, then progressively take ownership of new subject area datamarts
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Progressive ownership of the modeling layer is strategic, but much of the work is framed as gathering requirements and presenting/validating with stakeholders, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role: Experience using n8n to automate data workflows and event-driven triggers across GTM systems, with exposure to LLM-driven or agentic patterns
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected (as a preferred skill) to build automated data workflows with LLM-driven/agentic patterns, making AI downstream of the candidate's infrastructure work.
evidence.testing_framing: you build transformation models, write dbt tests, and design star and snowflake schemas from scratch
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Writing dbt tests is named as an owned, hands-on responsibility rather than a tool merely listed in a skills section.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: confirm analytical logic is sound, and incorporate feedback to improve reliability and self-serve adoption
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability and stakeholder trust in dashboards are a stated concern, but the framing is about adoption/reliability, not compliance or regulatory risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: extending our existing dimensional models before progressively taking ownership of new datamart development
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes extending existing dimensional models with designing new fact/dimension tables and datamarts from scratch.
evidence.work_arrangement: We operate in a flexible work model that combines both in-person and remote work
evidence.loss_aversion: confirm analytical logic is sound, and incorporate feedback to improve reliability and self-serve adoption — reflects concern for dashboard reliability and adoption rather than compliance risk.
